title: pouzolzia zeylanica var. angustifolia
intro: Pouzolzia zeylanica var. angustifolia, also known as Indian nettle, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Urticaceae, characterized by its small, white or greenish flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihistaminic effects. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. Pouzolzia zeylanica var. angustifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
